question: Which set of operations does the Orchestration Cluster REST API let you perform on a Camunda 8 cluster?

options.text: Start, manage, and query process instances; complete user tasks; resolve incidents; and manage variables.
Read-only access to process and task data — write operations require the Java client.
Only deploy BPMN definitions; runtime interaction must go through the Operate UI.
Only complete user tasks; process instance lifecycle requires a separate Tasklist API.

a.text: The docs describe the API as a programmatic interface for process orchestration: starting, managing, and querying process instances, completing user tasks, resolving incidents, and managing variables — all at scale.

b.text: The API is explicitly write-capable; start, complete, and resolve operations are part of its core surface.

c.text: Deploying BPMN is one capability, but runtime orchestration (instances, tasks, incidents, variables) is also covered by the same API.

d.text: User task completion is included, but so is the full process lifecycle; there is no separate Tasklist API requirement for these operations.

explanation: The Orchestration Cluster REST API is the programmatic front door to Camunda 8's process orchestration capabilities. It covers the full lifecycle — start, manage, and query process instances; complete user tasks; resolve incidents; manage variables — and it is what enables process-driven applications, custom user task UIs, and external systems to start and monitor processes.
